diagrams.title: Three-Level DBMS Architecture (ANSI-SPARC)
diagrams.description: Shows the three-schema architecture with external, conceptual and internal levels connected through mapping layers
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how different levels of abstraction are connected and how data independence is achieved

diagrams.title: File System vs DBMS Comparison
diagrams.description: Side-by-side comparison showing disadvantages of file system and advantages of DBMS approach
elements: File System Issues: Redundancy, Inconsistency, Security Risk, No Recovery, Concurrent Access Problems
DBMS Solutions: Normalization, Constraints, Authorization, Logging, Locking
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing why DBMS is preferred over traditional file processing

diagrams.title: DBMS Components Architecture
diagrams.description: Shows how different components of DBMS interact with each other and with users
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the modular architecture of DBMS and how components coordinate
